session_log_buffer.h(3) Library Functions Manual session_log_buffer.h(3)

NAME

session_log_buffer.h - Thread-safe circular log buffer for session screens (status, splash).

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct session_log_entry_t
Single log entry with sequence number for ordering.

Macros


#define SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE 8192
#define SESSION_LOG_LINE_MAX 1024

Functions


session_log_buffer_t * session_log_buffer_create (void)
Create a new session log buffer instance. void session_log_buffer_destroy (session_log_buffer_t *buf)
Cleanup session log buffer instance. void session_log_buffer_clear (session_log_buffer_t *buf)
Clear all log entries from buffer. void session_log_buffer_append (session_log_buffer_t *buf, const char *message)
Append a log message to the buffer. size_t session_log_buffer_get_recent (session_log_buffer_t *buf, session_log_entry_t *out_entries, size_t max_count)
Get recent log entries from buffer.

Detailed Description

Thread-safe circular log buffer for session screens (status, splash).

Provides a reusable log capture mechanism for session screens that need to display logs alongside fixed header content without terminal scrolling.

Thread-safe for concurrent append operations. Designed for use by:

  • Server status screen (live log feed under status header)
  • Splash screen (logs under animated ASCII art)

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Function Documentation

void session_log_buffer_append (session_log_buffer_t * buf, const char * message)

Append a log message to the buffer. Thread-safe. Called from logging system to capture messages. Message is copied into circular buffer (oldest entry overwritten if full).

Parameters

buf Buffer instance (must not be NULL)
message Log message text (already formatted with colors)

Definition at line 88 of file platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.

88                                                                                {
89   (void)buf;
90   (void)message;
91   // No-op - session log buffer not used in WASM
92 }




References atomic_fetch_add_u64(),
    atomic_load_u64(), atomic_store_u64(),
    session_log_buffer::entries, session_log_entry_t::message,
    session_log_buffer::mutex, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    SAFE_STRNCPY, session_log_buffer::sequence,
    session_log_entry_t::sequence, SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE,
    SESSION_LOG_LINE_MAX, and session_log_buffer::write_pos.






void
  session_log_buffer_clear (session_log_buffer_t * buf)


Clear all log entries from buffer. Thread-safe. Resets buffer to
    empty state, discarding all captured logs. Useful for clearing
    initialization logs before screen starts rendering.




Parameters


buf Buffer instance (must not be NULL)


Definition at line 83 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


83                                                          {
84   (void)buf;
85   // No-op - session log buffer not used in WASM
86 }

    session_log_buffer::entries, session_log_entry_t::message,
    session_log_buffer::mutex, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    session_log_buffer::sequence, session_log_entry_t::sequence,
    SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE, and
  session_log_buffer::write_pos.


Referenced by terminal_screen_log_clear().






session_log_buffer_t * session_log_buffer_create (void
  )


Create a new session log buffer instance. Allocates and
    initializes a circular buffer for log capture. Caller is responsible for
    calling session_log_buffer_destroy() when done.




Returns


Pointer to new buffer instance, or NULL on allocation
  failure


Definition at line 74 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


74                                                       {
75   return NULL;  // No-op - session log buffer not used in WASM
76 }

    session_log_buffer::mutex, mutex_init(), SAFE_CALLOC,
    session_log_buffer::sequence, and
    session_log_buffer::write_pos.


Referenced by terminal_screen_log_init().






void
  session_log_buffer_destroy (session_log_buffer_t * buf)


Cleanup session log buffer instance. Frees the buffer and all
    internal resources. Safe to call with NULL.




Parameters


buf Buffer instance to destroy (can be NULL)


Definition at line 78 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


78                                                            {
79   (void)buf;
80   // No-op - session log buffer not used in WASM
81 }






References session_log_buffer::mutex,
    mutex_destroy(), and SAFE_FREE.


Referenced by terminal_screen_log_destroy().






size_t
  session_log_buffer_get_recent (session_log_buffer_t * buf,
  session_log_entry_t * out_entries, size_t max_count)


Get recent log entries from buffer. Thread-safe. Copies up to
    max_count recent entries into out_entries. Entries are returned in
    chronological order (oldest first).




Parameters


buf Buffer instance (must not be NULL)


out_entries Output array to fill with log entries


max_count Maximum number of entries to return


Returns


Number of entries actually copied (may be less than
  max_count)


Definition at line 94 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/log.c.


96                                                         {
97   (void)buf;
98   (void)out_entries;
99   (void)max_count;
100   return 0; // No entries in WASM
101 }

    session_log_buffer::entries, session_log_buffer::mutex,
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock, session_log_buffer::sequence,
    session_log_entry_t::sequence, SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE,
    session_log_buffer::write_pos, and write_pos.


Referenced by log_search_gather_and_filter_logs(), and
